fields.narrative_intro: *A flabbergasted person and a fuming person discuss US AI strategy inside a customer support chatbot that has completely given up.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
FLABBERGASTED PERSON: "80% of startups are on Chinese models now."
FUMING PERSON: "Because of the export controls we put on China."
fields.panel2:
[build]
FLABBERGASTED PERSON: "We blocked their GPUs so they'd fall behind."
FUMING PERSON: "So they went open-weights instead."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
FLABBERGASTED PERSON: "And open-weights became the distribution advantage."
FUMING PERSON: "Qwen. Kimi K3. Free. Everywhere."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Both characters stare forward in total silence. The chatbot's interface behind them displays: 'Your issue is important to us. Estimated wait: ∞'. A small tumbleweed rolls across the screen.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
FUMING PERSON: "We kneecapped ourselves to kneecap them."
FLABBERGASTED PERSON: "Per a16z. Citing The Economist."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The chatbot's auto-response fires: 'Have you tried switching to Qwen? It's a fraction of the cost.' A tiny American flag in the corner droops.']

narrative_summary: Two characters inside a broken chatbot slowly realize the US GPU export controls designed to hurt China accidentally handed Chinese open-weights models a global distribution win — with the chatbot itself cheerfully suggesting Qwen as the solution.
rationale: Candidate 1 earns the win on punchline and specificity: the panel-5 attribution ('Per a16z. Citing The Economist.') is a genuinely funny deadpan citation drop that rewards readers who know the story, and the panel-6 button — the chatbot itself recommending Qwen while a tiny American flag droops — is a tight, earned visual that doesn't just restate the setup but adds a new layer of irony. The other candidates share the same skeleton but their closing beats either repeat the premise or reach for whimsy without the same payoff.
story_title: China's open-weights AI strategy is winning
place: inside a customer support chatbot that has completely given up
hat_pair: foam storm cloud hat with a dangling plastic lightning bolt
foam neural network diagram worn as a helmet with blinking nodes
